sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 11.49 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.11)

statement.latex: \,B.\,Hartley (Proc. London Math. Soc., 35, no.\,1 (1977), 55--75) constructed an example of a non-countable Artinian${ Z}G$-module where$G$is a met\-abe\-lian group with the minimum condition for normal subgroups. It follows that there exists a non-countable soluble group (of derived length 3) satisfying Min-$n$. The following question arises in connection with this result and with the study of s...
